authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 436 - Disclosure Requirements and Prohibitions Concerning Franchising
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The federal franchise rule is the primary U.S. regulatory baseline for FDD disclosures, Item 19 financial performance representations, Item 20 outlet-status tables, and prohibited franchise-sales conduct that constrain unit performance reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: franchise disclosure baseline and unit-record qualification

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC staff guidance explains reasonable basis, written substantiation, outlet-specific evidence handling, and update expectations under the Franchise Rule.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: financial performance substantiation and disclosure-control review

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Resources
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA is the coordinating body for state franchise administrators and publishes official franchise resources, guidelines, commentaries, and state-information references used in registration-state workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: state-review workflow and franchise resource qualification

authoritative_sources.title: NASAA Franchise Commentary: Financial Performance Representations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's FPR commentary is recognized state-regulator guidance for averages, subsets, outlet samples, reasonable basis, and misleading financial performance representation risk.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: unit metric definition, cohort, and substantiation review
used_for: FPR evidence sufficiency and refusal triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Multi-Unit Commentary
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's multi-unit commentary directly addresses area development, subfranchise, and area representative structures that change unit-count, commitment, territory, and portfolio-performance interpretation.

authoritative_sources.title: Disclosing Financial Performance Representations in the Time of COVID-19
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's historical FPR guidance explains how material changes can make prior performance data stale and why system-specific facts are required before historical unit performance claims remain supportable.
used_for: stronger-claim refusal when operating conditions shift

authoritative_sources.title: Commission Guidance on Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is the authoritative public baseline for clear metric definitions, calculations, estimates, changed methodology, and caveats when unit performance metrics are prepared for formal reporting audiences.

authoritative_sources.title: Common Franchising Terms and Definitions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IFA is the principal U.S. franchising trade association. Its glossary provides recognized terminology for gross revenue, royalty fee, company-owned location, field consultant, area franchisee, FDD, and FPR vocabulary after the regulatory baseline is set.
used_for: field consultant and FPR term alignment

workflow_stages.name: source collection and unit-context qualification
workflow_stages.description: Collect current FDD edition, Item 19 support, Item 20 outlet status, unit master, franchise agreement context, jurisdiction scope, intended audience, and tenant source hierarchy before metric work begins.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: metric definition and cohort logic
workflow_stages.description: Resolve AUV, same-store sales, comp sales, gross sales, royalty base, ad-fund contribution, labor, audit, opening-ramp, transfer, closure, and maturity denominator rules.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: record collection and cross-system validation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile unit master, POS, royalty, ERP, franchisee reporting, field audit, Item 19, and Item 20 records to determine whether requested performance metrics can be supported confidently.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: unit analysis and report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Build a unit scorecard, variance brief, or benchmark package with explicit source logic, refresh assumptions, thresholds, caveats, and confidence posture.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: narrative synthesis and disclosure-risk review
workflow_stages.description: Stop descriptive analysis from becoming unsupported earnings claims, forecasts, causal claims, legal interpretations, policy decisions, or public disclosure statements without named evidence and approvals.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception escalation and adjacent-authority routing
workflow_stages.description: Route stale data, source conflicts, state-law context, royalty approvals, field mandates, development remedies, and disclosure review cases to the correct specialist with evidence packaging.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: current and prior FDDs, state addenda, effective dates, and receipt or approval records
Item 19 substantiation files, supporting schedules, cohort definitions, and approval notes
Item 20 outlet-status tables covering openings, transfers, reacquisitions, closures, renewals, terminations, and projected openings
unit master records with ownership, operating status, open date, closure date, transfer date, maturity, geography, brand, territory, and franchisee group
franchise agreements, area development agreements, transfer records, renewal records, and agreement-specific gross-sales definitions
POS sales, transaction, ticket, traffic, discount, product mix, labor, inventory, and local marketing records
royalty-reported gross sales, royalty invoices, ad-fund assessments, ERP or GL postings, credits, and finance close calendars
field consulting notes, brand-standard audit scores, action plans, support tickets, training records, and remediation status
metric dictionaries, source-precedence rules, reporting calendars, disclosure approvals, caveat notes, and reconciliation packages
systems_of_record: franchise management or franchise CRM platform
unit master or territory management system
POS or franchise sales data warehouse
royalty billing or accounts receivable platform
BI or reporting warehouse and metric catalog
field operations or brand-standards audit system
learning or training management system when training signals affect performance interpretation
document control and disclosure approval repository

allowed: Classify unit performance requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Define or validate unit performance metrics, source hierarchy, denominator logic, cohort rules, refresh assumptions, and caveats.
Prepare unit scorecards, variance briefs, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk escalations without overstating legal, royalty, field, or sales authority.
Record tenant-specific metric adaptations while preserving public baseline assumptions and named caveats.
escalate: Required tenant facts, FDD edition context, Item 19 support, Item 20 status, unit master, agreement definitions, jurisdiction scope, or source-precedence rules are missing.
POS, royalty, ERP, franchisee reporting, field audit, unit master, or disclosure records remain stale, incomplete, or conflicting after one reconciliation pass.
State registration posture, franchise-law interpretation, public disclosure, franchise-sales use, royalty approval, field action authority, or development remedy materially changes the answer.
Requests cross into Item 19 revision, state filing preparation, legal interpretation, royalty concession approval, transfer or termination decisions, field mandates, or formal public signoff.
refuse: Requests to draft, revise, or approve Item 19 language, FDDs, state filings, franchise-sales materials, or public disclosures.
Requests to promise or imply franchisee earnings, AUV attainment, payback, profit, sales lift, renewal likelihood, or franchisee success.
Requests to approve royalty abatements, ad-fund credits, transfer remedies, termination actions, development-default remedies, or field-consulting mandates.
Requests to bypass franchise-specific ownership, agreement, unit-status, disclosure, and cohort controls in favor of generic performance reporting.

domain_failure_modes: Mixing franchised and company-owned units in AUV, same-store sales, or benchmarks without explicit cohort treatment.
Treating signed development commitments, sold territories, or subfranchise rights as operating unit performance.
Using stale Item 19 support or historical benchmark data after material changes in unit mix, ownership, operating model, or market conditions.
Assuming POS gross sales, royalty-reported gross sales, ERP invoices, and franchisee-submitted values are interchangeable despite timing and agreement-definition differences.
Counting transferred, reacquired, temporarily closed, immature, or terminated units as stable peers without tenant cohort rules.
Turning field consulting, audit-score, training, or support-ticket correlation into causal claims about sales lift, renewals, or franchisee success.
Publishing internal unit scorecards as if they were legally approved Item 19, lender, investor, or prospect-facing disclosures.
Using the industry label cosmetically and producing a generic retail dashboard that ignores FDD, agreement, ownership, and outlet-status records.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane is governed by current FDD editions, Item 19 substantiation, Item 20 outlet-status tables, unit master records, franchise and development agreements, jurisdiction and filing posture, POS records, royalty and ad-fund ledgers, ERP postings, field audit systems, tenant metric dictionaries, and disclosure approval artifacts.

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: Unit performance work fails when it ignores franchised versus company-owned status, uses stale FPR support, treats development commitments as operating units, collapses POS and royalty definitions, ignores transfers or closures, or turns internal scorecards into earnings claims or field mandates. These are franchise-specific risks beyond generic analytics.

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane stops at descriptive metric definition, source lineage, scorecard assembly, caveat writing, reconciliation, and escalation packaging. Franchise compliance, legal review, state filing preparation, royalty or ad-fund concessions, field consulting mandates, franchise development remedies, finance approvals, and public disclosure signoff belong to adjacent specialists or human approvers.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State franchise registration, relationship, transfer, renewal, and termination rules vary by jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline uses FTC and NASAA sources, but state-specific legal variation must be supplied as tenant jurisdiction context and routed to compliance or legal review before disclosure-sensitive conclusions are treated as safe.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant franchise agreements and metric dictionaries often define gross sales, royalty exclusions, comparable cohorts, temporary closures, and transferred-unit treatment differently.
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources constrain evidence and disclosure posture, but tenant-specific agreement language and metric rules are mandatory runtime inputs before final cohort or royalty-sensitive conclusions are inferred.

notes: Research constrains the lane to franchise-specific unit performance evidence, disclosure records, agreement definitions, outlet status, royalty/POS reconciliation, and multi-unit context.
No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain for a validated spec pack because jurisdiction-specific and agreement-specific variation is handled as a required runtime input and escalation condition.
